content:
We have some doubts about multiples module fields on module! macro.
The authors field acctuatly only accept a one author:

module! {
    // another fields
   author: "author"
}

while, the firmware and alias fields accept multiples values with a array, like:

module! {
    // another fields
    alias: ["alias_1", "alias_2"],
    firmware: ["firmware_1", "firmware_2"]
}

We need the macro module! accept multiple authors too...

@Miguel Ojeda was have suggestion the follow ways for this:

- A single field, that only accepts a list.
Like alias and firmware.

- A single field that accepts both a string or a list.
We will need change the alias and firmware for consistence

- Two fields (author and authors)
cannot coexist.
We will need change the alias and firmware for consistence

- Accepting several "author" fields and append them all into a list.
like already is doing in .c side

The initial discussion can be found here
@Daniel Sedlak

@Miguel Ojeda @Daniel Sedlak
How the nobody answer this topic, we can follow with the multiples fields in module! macro ? like:

module! {
    // anothers fields
    author: "a1",
    author: "a2",
    // ...
    author: "an",
}

This for author, alias and firmware.
I'm fine to making this change and sent patches

thoughts ?

Jumping in, maybe I'm not the right person but I'll share my thoughts:

I'd say to maintain consistency and reduce verbosity, I suggest adopting a single authors field that always takes an array. This keeps the design consistent with how alias and firmware are already handled, avoids introducing multiple author fields.

To tackle formatting inconsistencies with arrays, we could:

  • Define formatting guidelines in the Rust kernel style documentation.
  • Implement checks in checkpatch.pl to enforce proper formatting.

Maybe we can keep the existing author as deprecated? For as long as needed.

@Charalampos Mitrodimas wrote:

I'd say to maintain consistency and reduce verbosity, I suggest adopting a
single authors field that always takes an array. This keeps the design consistent with
how alias and firmware are already handled, avoids introducing
multiple author fields.

Yes, the single authors fields reduce verbosity and avoid scrolling the screen in modules that has a firmwre, authors or alias a lot.
On the other hand, they are not many modules with a large amount of firmware and alias fields. The marjority has one or two authors, firmware and alias.
You can run this command for analyses:
grep -rwo 'MODULE_FIRMWARE' . | awk -F: '{count[$1]++} END {for (file in count) if (count[file] > 1) print file, count[file]}' | sort -k2 -n > res

Maybe we can keep the existing author as deprecated? For as long as needed.

I think that we don't need set the author as deprecated because we don't have modules a lot that call the module! macro. We can change the all modules that call the module! macro easily.

@Charalampos Mitrodimas
And the @Daniel Sedlak argued in favor of repeating fields:

Daniel wrote:

Since module is a macro, if we would allow syntax in the macro like:

authors: ["author1", "author2", ...]

I think we could fight with the code formatting, because when it comes
to the rust macros, rustfmt is often very confused and we could end up
with variations like:

authors: ["author1", "author2",
"author3"]

or

authors: [
"author1",
"author2",
]

and rustfmt would be totally ok with both of them.

Miguel wrote:

We could also try to mitigate the formatting issue via e.g.
checkpatch.pl if needed.

Daniel wrote:

That is true, it could be part of checkpatch.pl, however I would argue
that if we can overcame the formatting problems by repeating the field,
instead of modifying checkpatch.pl, then none code is better than some
code (regarding modifying checkpatch.pl).

Guilherme Giácomo Simões said:

I think that we don't need set the author as deprecated because we don't have modules a lot that call the module! macro. We can change the all modules that call the module! macro easily.

Yes makes sense.

Should you send a patch and let the discussion happen there?
@Charalampos Mitrodimas l already sent a pacth https://lore.kernel.org/all/20241206192244.443486-1-trintaeoitogc@gmail.com/

I'm back to this task, and analyzing the solutions, I can noticed that in the let info = ModuleInfo::parse(&mut it); the parse() return a ModuleInfo struct.
This struct obviously don't have multiples fields to authors, firmware or alias. And , once that the parse of ModuleInfo is neccessary, I don't know how we can do module! macro has multiples fields.

The ModuleInfo::parse() will exclude one or more of multiples fields.

If nobody was have any way for make this with multiples fielda way, I will submite a patch with authors as a Array ....

I will make some changes in module.rs , and change the author to authors... But I'm not happy with:

authors: ["author_1", "author_2", "author_3",
    "author_4", "author5"]

This seemed better to me before. I maked a change in checkpatch for analyzing this keys and limite for three values per line.
But NOW, I'm think that is better this:

authors: [
    "author_1",
    "author_2",
    "author3"
]

Exactly the thing that I don't was want. For me, boring scrolling is terrible. But, we don't have a lot of modules with a lot of author, firmware or alias.

I change the module.rs for accepted authors as a array, and after tests I make a change in checkpatch.pl with the way that we decided.

@Charalampos Mitrodimas
@Miguel Ojeda
@Daniel Sedlak

Guilherme Giácomo Simões said:

I will make some changes in module.rs , and change the author to authors... But I'm not happy with:

authors: ["author_1", "author_2", "author_3",
    "author_4", "author5"]

This seemed better to me before. I maked a change in checkpatch for analyzing this keys and limite for three values per line.
But NOW, I'm think that is better this:

authors: [
    "author_1",
    "author_2",
    "author3"
]

Can we end up with something like this? 

authors: [
    "author_1", "author_2", "author_3",
    "author_4", "author_5", "author_6"
]

IMO go on with A single field, that only accepts a list. as mentioned by Miguel. Also pinging @wcampbell because of this PR.

@Charalampos Mitrodimas said:

Can we end up with something like this?

authors: [
"author_1", "author_2", "author_3",
"author_4", "author_5", "author_6"
]

So, I made a change to the checkpatch to limit the number of fields on the same line to 3, exactly how do you exemplified.

I am not sure several authors look better in a single line or fit, since they have emails and so on (i.e. they are not short strings as those above), and anyway for e.g. commit tags we use one per line too. C also uses one per line.

e.g.

MODULE_AUTHOR("Antoine Tenart <antoine.tenart@free-electrons.com>");
MODULE_AUTHOR("Ofer Heifetz <oferh@marvell.com>");
MODULE_AUTHOR("Igal Liberman <igall@marvell.com>");

Miguel Ojeda said:

I am not sure several authors look better in a single line or fit, since they have emails and so on (i.e. they are not short strings as those above), and anyway for e.g. commit tags we use one per line too. C also uses one per line.

but I can noticed that in the let info = ModuleInfo::parse(&mut it); in module.rs the parse() return a ModuleInfo struct.
This struct obviously don't have multiples fields to authors, firmware or alias. And , once that the parse of ModuleInfo is neccessary, I don't know how we can do module! macro has multiples fields.

The ModuleInfo::parse() will exclude one or more of multiples fields.

How do this ?

@Miguel Ojeda
So .. Maybe is valid we do:

authors: [
    "author_1",
    "author_2",
    "author3"
]
Sorry, what do you mean multiple fields? We are discussing on having one authors, correct?

Guilherme Giácomo Simões said:

So .. Maybe is valid we do:

authors: [
    "author_1",
    "author_2",
    "author3"
]

To me that looks perfectly fine. If there is more than 1 author, I don't see why we would do something else...

If there is a single author, then I think will probably want to do authors: [ "..." ], so possibly we shouldn't warn about that (personally I wouldn't mind the warning even then).
Also, I personally don't care too much if we allow a single author with authors: "..." or not.

@Miguel Ojeda
@Charalampos Mitrodimas
@Daniel Sedlak

@Guilherme Giácomo Simões The patch itself does what it has to do. Rest of the reviewers will also tell.

I noticed some typos and grammar/syntax issues in important places, such as the patch description and checkpatch.el changes. Do you think you can take care of those yourself? If not, let me know what would be the best method for me to help you.

@Charalampos Mitrodimas
My english is not so good, so, if the mistakes of grammar/syntax is about english, maybe I really need a help.

You can reply the PATCHs , and say for me the fixs and I make the changes.

Thougths ?
I seeing that you saing about for author in authors , and about the rust_driver_platform.rs , I can do this changes together the fixing grammar mistakes and submite a v3

Guilherme Giácomo Simões said:

Charalampos Mitrodimas
My english is not so good, so, if the mistakes of grammar/syntax is about english, maybe I really need a help.

No worries, a lot of us are non-native speakers too.

@Charalampos Mitrodimas
I changed the commit messages:

    rust: module: change author to be a array

    In the module! macro, the author field has a string type. Since modules
    can have more than one author, this is currently impossible.
    Change the author field to the type Option<Vec<String>> to allow
    creating modules with multiple authors. Additionally, rename the field
    from author to authors to make it explicit that it can refer to multiple
    authors.
    In modules that use the author field, update its value to an array of
    strings, and also rename it from author to authors.

and for checkpatch change:

    checkpatch: throw error in malformed arrays

    In the module! macro, some fields have a Vec<String> type, and we need a
    check to ensure proper formatting.
    Check if the current line contains one of these keys that should be of
    type Vec<String>, and if so, use a regex to verify if it contains more
    than one value in the array. If it does, the array should be formatted
    vertically.
    If the array has only one value, then it can stay on the same line

maybe this is better than previous messages .. I used DeepSeek for translate.
The warnings in checkpatch looks like this:

Prefer a single-line value
...
Prefer declare ] on the same line
...
Prefer a new line after the last value and before ]
...
Prefer a new line after [

the diff of checkpatch:

diff --git a/scripts/checkpatch.pl b/scripts/checkpatch.pl
index 7b28ad331742..47e081398bde 100755
--- a/scripts/checkpatch.pl
+++ b/scripts/checkpatch.pl
@@ -2775,6 +2775,9 @@ sub process {
        $realcnt = 0;
        $linenr = 0;
        $fixlinenr = -1;
+
+       my %array_parse_module;
+
        foreach my $line (@lines) {
                $linenr++;
                $fixlinenr++;
@@ -3567,6 +3570,46 @@ sub process {
 # ignore non-hunk lines and lines being removed
                next if (!$hunk_line || $line =~ /^-/);

+# check if arrays has more than one value in the same line
+               my $inline = 0;
+               my $key = "";
+               my $add_line = $line =~ /^\+/;
+
+               if ($line =~ /\s*.*\b(author|alias|firmware)\s*:\s*\[/) {
+                       $inline = 1;
+                       $array_parse_module{$1} = 1;
+               }
+
+               my @keys = keys %array_parse_module;
+               if (@keys) {
+                       $key = $keys[0];
+               }
+
+               if ($add_line && $key) {
+                       my $herevet = "$here\n" . cat_vet($rawline) . "\n";
+
+                       my $counter = () = $line =~ /"/g;
+                       my $more_than_one = $counter > 2;
+                       if ($more_than_one) {
+                               WARN("ARRAY_MODULE_MACRO",
+                                        "Prefer a single-line value$herevet");
+                       } elsif ($inline && $line !~ /\]/ && $line !~ /,/ && $line =~ /"/) {
+                               WARN("ARRAY_MODULE_MACRO",
+                                        "Prefer declare ] on the same line$herevet");
+                       } elsif (!$inline && $line =~ /\]/ && $line =~ /\"/) {
+                               WARN("ARRAY_MODULE_MACRO",
+                                        "Prefer a new line after the last value and before ]$herevet");
+                       } elsif ($inline && $line =~ /,/ && $line !~ /\]/) {
+                               WARN("ARRAY_MODULE_MACRO",
+                                        "Prefer a new line after [$herevet");
+                       }
+               }
+
+               #END OF ANALYZE FIELD
+               if ($line =~ /\]/) {
+                       delete $array_parse_module{$key};
+               }
+
 #trailing whitespace
                if ($line =~ /^\+.*\015/) {
                        my $herevet = "$here\n" . cat_vet($rawline) . "\n";

Too, I changes the author field to authors ... And anothers things that is asked in mailing list.

What do you think, is better ?
I can sent a v3 patch

What do you think about these?

rust: module: change author to an array

In the module! macro, the author field is currently of type
String. Since modules can have multiple authors, this limitation
prevents specifying more than one.  Change the author field to
Option<Vec<String>> to allow creating modules with multiple
authors. Additionally, rename the field from author to authors to make
it explicit that it can refer to multiple authors.  In modules that use
the author field, update its value to an array of strings, and also
rename it from author to authors.

checkpatch: throw error for malformed arrays

Implement a check to ensure these fields are correctly formatted. If a
line contains one of these keys that should be of type Vec<String>, use
a regex to verify whether the array holds multiple values.
  * If the array contains more than one value, enforce vertical formatting
  * If the array has only one value, it can remain on the same line

For checkpatch changes, I'm really not the person to judge perl... On-list review would be better for sure.
And to quote Andreas, I'm also recommending b4, it will make your patch sending much easier and keep track of stuff you might not know you have to.

Charalampos Mitrodimas said:

What do you think about these?

rust: module: change author to an array

In the module! macro, the author field is currently of type
String. Since modules can have multiple authors, this limitation
prevents specifying more than one.  Change the author field to
Option<Vec<String>> to allow creating modules with multiple
authors. Additionally, rename the field from author to authors to make
it explicit that it can refer to multiple authors.  In modules that use
the author field, update its value to an array of strings, and also
rename it from author to authors.

checkpatch: throw error for malformed arrays

Implement a check to ensure these fields are correctly formatted. If a
line contains one of these keys that should be of type Vec<String>, use
a regex to verify whether the array holds multiple values.
  * If the array contains more than one value, enforce vertical formatting
  * If the array has only one value, it can remain on the same line

For checkpatch changes, I'm really not the person to judge perl... On-list review would be better for sure.

For me, looks like great, I will send a v3

@Charalampos Mitrodimas @Miguel Ojeda @Daniel Sedlak

V3
